title: arum carsaamii
intro: Arum carsaamii, also known as the "Greater Celandine", is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the family Papaveraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate the liver and gallbladder, acting as a choleretic agent. The health benefits of Arum carsaamii include its potential to protect against liver and gallbladder diseases, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Arum carsaamii is often prepared in herbal tinctures, infusions, or topical creams, and is also used in traditional folk remedies for skin and eye issues.
